meaning: adj.涓嶄簡瑙ｇ殑,涓嶈皡瑙ｇ殑
examples: He gave the bottle a long, uncomprehending look.
Cersei stared at him, uncomprehending. "What woman?"
In the days that followed, I learned to spell in this uncomprehending way many words.
When she dies, apparently in an accident, her loving and uncomprehending husband is forced to retrace her steps and rekindle some of her fire.
He looked uncomprehending at his colleagues and remained in position.
